   128→# Cross-platform date from timestamp
   129→date_from_timestamp() {

   228→push_changes() {

   232→    
   233→    while [[ $retry -lt $max_retries ]]; do
   234→        if git push origin "$branch" 2>&1; then
   235→            return 0
   236→        fi

   238→        retry=$((retry + 1))

   254→# Run Claude CLI with retry logic for transient failures
   255→run_claude_with_retry() {
   256→    local prompt_file="$1"
   257→    local output_file="$2"
   258→    local mode="$3"
   259→    local retry=0
   260→    local exit_code

   262→    # Determine if we should use pretty output formatting

   276→                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
   277→            else
   278→                envsubst < "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
   279→                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
   280→                    --output-format=stream-json \
   281→                    --model "$MODEL" \
   282→                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
   283→                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
   284→            fi
   285→        else
   286→            if [[ "$use_formatter" == "true" ]]; then
   287→                cat "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
   288→                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
   289→                    --output-format=stream-json \
   290→                    --model "$MODEL" \
   291→                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file" | "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT"
   292→                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
   293→            else
   294→                cat "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
   295→                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
   296→                    --output-format=stream-json \
   297→                    --model "$MODEL" \
   298→                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
   299→                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
   300→            fi

   302→
   303→        # Check for transient errors that should be retried
   304→        if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
   305→            return 0
   306→        fi
   307→
   308→        # Check if it's a retryable error
   309→        if grep -qE "ETIMEDOUT|ECONNRESET|ECONNREFUSED|503|504|rate.?limit" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
   310→            retry=$((retry + 1))
   311→            if [[ $retry -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
   312→                log_warn "Transient error detected. Retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s... (attempt $((retry + 1))/$MAX_RETRIES)"
   313→                sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
   314→                continue
   315→            fi
   316→        fi
   317→
   318→        # Non-retryable error or max retries reached
   319→        return $exit_code
   320→    done
   321→
   322→    return 1
   323→}

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# Ralph Wiggum Loop - Production Hardened Version 2.1
# =============================================================================
# Based on Geoffrey Huntley's ORIGINAL method (external loop, clean context)
# 
# CRITICAL FIXES v2.1:
# 1. Claude CLI timeout configuration (CRITICAL - prevents 2-min kill)
# 2. API timeout configuration
# 3. Retry logic for transient failures
# 4. Cross-platform date compatibility (Linux/macOS)
# 5. bc dependency fallback
# 6. Fixed variable scope issues
# 7. Improved error handling
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

# =============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"

# Source circuit breaker
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh" ]]; then
    source "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh"
    CB_ENABLED=true
else
    CB_ENABLED=false
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} circuit-breaker.sh not found - running without protection"
fi

# Ralph files
PROMPT_BUILD="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_build.md"
PROMPT_PLAN="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_plan.md"
PROMPT_PLAN_WORK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/PROMPT_plan_work.md"
AGENTS_FILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/AGENTS.md"
IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN="${SCRIPT_DIR}/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"

# Output formatter (optional, for human-readable output)
FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/scripts/format-output.sh"
PRETTY_OUTPUT=${PRETTY_OUTPUT:-true}

# Default model
DEFAULT_MODEL="opus"

# Rate limiting
MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR=${MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR:-9999999}
CALLS_FILE="/tmp/ralph-calls-$(date +%Y%m%d%H)"

# Output capture
OUTPUT_DIR="/tmp/ralph-outputs-$$"
mkdir -p "$OUTPUT_DIR"

# =============================================================================
# TIMEOUT CONFIGURATION (CRITICAL)
# =============================================================================
# Claude CLI has a default 2-minute timeout on bash commands.
# This will kill long-running builds, tests, and optimizer runs!
# Configure these environment variables to extend timeouts.
#
# Reference: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/5615
# =============================================================================

# Bash command timeout (ms) - default 30 minutes
export BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=${BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS:-1800000}
# Maximum bash timeout (ms) - default 2 hours
export BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS=${BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS:-7200000}
# API request timeout (ms) - default 10 minutes
export API_TIMEOUT_MS=${API_TIMEOUT_MS:-600000}
# MCP server startup timeout (ms) - default 30 seconds
export MCP_TIMEOUT=${MCP_TIMEOUT:-30000}
# Disable non-essential traffic (improves stability)
export CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC=${CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC:-1}

# Retry configuration for Claude CLI calls
MAX_RETRIES=${MAX_RETRIES:-3}
RETRY_DELAY=${RETRY_DELAY:-5}

# Completion signals to detect
COMPLETION_SIGNALS=(
    "ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE"
    "PLAN_COMPLETE"
    "EXIT_SIGNAL"
    "<promise>DONE</promise>"
    "<promise>COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>ALL_TASKS_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>PLAN_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>FIX_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>RALPH_BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETE</promise>"
    "<promise>BLOCKED</promise>"
)

# =============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

log_info() { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
log_success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"; }
log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
log_error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }

print_banner() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}"
    echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║     ██████╗  █████╗ ██╗     ██████╗ ██╗  ██╗                     ║"
    echo "║     ██╔══██╗██╔══██╗██║     ██╔══██╗██║  ██║                     ║"
    echo "║     ██████╔╝███████║██║     ██████╔╝███████║                     ║"
    echo "║     ██╔══██╗██╔══██║██║     ██╔═══╝ ██╔══██║                     ║"
    echo "║     ██║  ██║██║  ██║███████╗██║     ██║  ██║                     ║"
    echo "║     ╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝  ╚═╝╚══════╝╚═╝     ╚═╝  ╚═╝                     ║"
    echo "║                                                                  ║"
    echo "║     Metro Fleet Mileage Management System                        ║"
    echo "║     Production-Hardened Ralph Loop v2.1                          ║"
    echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo -e "${NC}"
}

# Cross-platform date from timestamp
date_from_timestamp() {
    local timestamp="$1"
    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU date (Linux)
        date -d "@$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    else
        # BSD date (macOS)
        date -r "$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    fi
}

check_rate_limit() {
    if [[ -f "$CALLS_FILE" ]]; then
        local calls
        calls=$(cat "$CALLS_FILE")
        if [[ $calls -ge $MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR ]]; then
            log_error "Rate limit reached: $MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR calls/hour"
            log_info "Reset in: $(( 60 - $(date +%M) )) minutes"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    return 0
}

increment_call_count() {
    local current=0
    if [[ -f "$CALLS_FILE" ]]; then
        current=$(cat "$CALLS_FILE")
    fi
    echo $((current + 1)) > "$CALLS_FILE"
}

check_prerequisites() {
    if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "Claude CLI not found. Install: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-cli"
        exit 1
    fi

    if ! git rev-parse --git-dir &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "Not in a git repository"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$AGENTS_FILE" ]]; then
        log_error "AGENTS.md not found at: $AGENTS_FILE"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check for jq (required for circuit breaker)
    if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
        log_warn "jq not found - circuit breaker features limited"
        log_info "Install with: apt install jq (Linux) or brew install jq (macOS)"
    fi
    
    # Check for envsubst (required for plan-work mode)
    if ! command -v envsubst &> /dev/null; then
        log_warn "envsubst not found - plan-work mode may not work"
        log_info "Install with: apt install gettext-base (Linux) or brew install gettext (macOS)"
    fi
}

check_completion_signal() {
    local output_file="$1"

    for signal in "${COMPLETION_SIGNALS[@]}"; do
        # Case-insensitive matching (-i) for robustness
        if grep -qi "$signal" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "$signal"
            return 0
        fi
    done

    return 1
}

check_for_errors() {
    local output_file="$1"
    
    # Check for JSON error blocks
    if grep -q '"type":"error"' "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        grep '"type":"error"' "$output_file" | head -1
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for common error patterns
    if grep -qE "FATAL|CRITICAL|Exception|Traceback" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        grep -E "FATAL|CRITICAL|Exception|Traceback" "$output_file" | head -1
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for API timeout errors
    if grep -qE "timeout|ETIMEDOUT|Request timed out" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        grep -E "timeout|ETIMEDOUT|Request timed out" "$output_file" | head -1
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1
}

push_changes() {
    local branch="$1"
    local max_retries=3
    local retry=0
    
    while [[ $retry -lt $max_retries ]]; do
        if git push origin "$branch" 2>&1; then
            return 0
        fi
        
        retry=$((retry + 1))
        
        if [[ $retry -eq 1 ]]; then
            log_warn "Push failed. Trying to create remote branch..."
            if git push -u origin "$branch" 2>&1; then
                return 0
            fi
        fi
        
        log_warn "Push attempt $retry/$max_retries failed. Retrying in 5s..."
        sleep 5
    done
    
    return 1
}

# Run Claude CLI with retry logic for transient failures
run_claude_with_retry() {
    local prompt_file="$1"
    local output_file="$2"
    local mode="$3"
    local retry=0
    local exit_code

    # Determine if we should use pretty output formatting
    local use_formatter=false
    if [[ "$PRETTY_OUTPUT" == "true" && -x "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT" ]]; then
        use_formatter=true
    fi

    while [[ $retry -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
        if [[ "$mode" == "plan-work" ]]; then
            if [[ "$use_formatter" == "true" ]]; then
                envsubst < "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file" | "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            else
                envsubst < "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            fi
        else
            if [[ "$use_formatter" == "true" ]]; then
                cat "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file" | "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            else
                cat "$prompt_file" | claude -p \
                    --dangerously-skip-permissions \
                    --output-format=stream-json \
                    --model "$MODEL" \
                    $VERBOSE 2>&1 | tee "$output_file"
                exit_code=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
            fi
        fi

        # Check for transient errors that should be retried
        if [[ $exit_code -eq 0 ]]; then
            return 0
        fi

        # Check if it's a retryable error
        if grep -qE "ETIMEDOUT|ECONNRESET|ECONNREFUSED|503|504|rate.?limit" "$output_file" 2>/dev/null; then
            retry=$((retry + 1))
            if [[ $retry -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
                log_warn "Transient error detected. Retrying in ${RETRY_DELAY}s... (attempt $((retry + 1))/$MAX_RETRIES)"
                sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
                continue
            fi
        fi

        # Non-retryable error or max retries reached
        return $exit_code
    done

    return 1
}

cleanup() {
    rm -rf "$OUTPUT_DIR"
    log_info "Cleaned up temporary files"
}

show_usage() {
    echo "Usage: ./loop.sh [MODE] [OPTIONS]"
    echo ""
    echo "Modes:"
    echo "  (none)              Build mode, unlimited iterations"
    echo "  <number>            Build mode, max N iterations"
    echo "  plan                Planning mode, unlimited"
    echo "  plan <number>       Planning mode, max N iterations"
    echo "  plan-work \"desc\"    Scoped planning for specific work"
    echo ""
    echo "Examples:"
    echo "  ./loop.sh                          # Build, unlimited"
    echo "  ./loop.sh 50                       # Build, max 50 iterations"
    echo "  ./loop.sh plan                     # Plan, unlimited"
    echo "  ./loop.sh plan 5                   # Plan, max 5 iterations"
    echo "  ./loop.sh plan-work \"ingestion\"    # Scoped plan"
    echo ""
    echo "Options:"
    echo "  -h, --help              Show this help"
    echo "  -m, --model MODEL       Use specific model (default: opus)"
    echo "  -v, --verbose           Verbose output"
    echo "  --no-push               Skip git push after iterations"
    echo "  --no-stop-on-complete   Continue even after completion signal"
    echo ""
    echo "Environment Variables:"
    echo "  MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR          Rate limit (default: 100)"
    echo "  CB_MAX_COST_USD             Circuit breaker cost limit (default: 50)"
    echo "  CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD    Iterations without commit (default: 3)"
    echo ""
    echo "Timeout Configuration (CRITICAL for long operations):"
    echo "  BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS     Bash command timeout (default: 1800000 = 30min)"
    echo "  BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS         Max bash timeout (default: 7200000 = 2hr)"
    echo "  API_TIMEOUT_MS              API request timeout (default: 600000 = 10min)"
    echo ""
    echo "Output:"
    echo "  PRETTY_OUTPUT               Enable formatted output (default: true)"
    echo "  --raw                       Disable output formatting (show raw JSON)"
    echo ""
    echo "Safety:"
    echo "  Ctrl+C                  Stop the loop"
    echo "  git reset --hard        Revert uncommitted changes"
    echo "  ./circuit-breaker.sh reset  Reset circuit breaker state"
}

# =============================================================================
# PARSE ARGUMENTS
# =============================================================================

MODE="build"
PROMPT_FILE="$PROMPT_BUILD"
MAX_ITERATIONS=0
WORK_DESCRIPTION=""
MODEL="$DEFAULT_MODEL"
VERBOSE=""
NO_PUSH=true
STOP_ON_COMPLETE=true

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
        -h|--help)
            show_usage
            exit 0
            ;;
        -m|--model)
            MODEL="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        -v|--verbose)
            VERBOSE="--verbose"
            shift
            ;;
        --no-push)
            NO_PUSH=true
            shift
            ;;
        --no-stop-on-complete)
            STOP_ON_COMPLETE=false
            shift
            ;;
        --raw)
            PRETTY_OUTPUT=false
            shift
            ;;
        plan)
            MODE="plan"
            PROMPT_FILE="$PROMPT_PLAN"
            shift
            if [[ $# -gt 0 && "$1" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
                MAX_ITERATIONS=$1
                shift
            fi
            ;;
        plan-work)
            MODE="plan-work"
            PROMPT_FILE="$PROMPT_PLAN_WORK"
            shift
            if [[ $# -gt 0 && ! "$1" =~ ^- ]]; then
                WORK_DESCRIPTION="$1"
                shift
            else
                log_error "plan-work requires a work description"
                echo "Usage: ./loop.sh plan-work \"description of work\""
                exit 1
            fi
            if [[ $# -gt 0 && "$1" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
                MAX_ITERATIONS=$1
                shift
            else
                MAX_ITERATIONS=5
            fi
            ;;
        [0-9]*)
            MAX_ITERATIONS=$1
            shift
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown option: $1"
            show_usage
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# =============================================================================
# VALIDATION
# =============================================================================

check_prerequisites

if [[ ! -f "$PROMPT_FILE" ]]; then
    log_error "Prompt file not found: $PROMPT_FILE"
    exit 1
fi

if [[ "$MODE" == "plan-work" ]]; then
    CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
    if [[ "$CURRENT_BRANCH" == "main" || "$CURRENT_BRANCH" == "master" ]]; then
        log_error "plan-work should be run on a work branch, not main/master"
        echo "Create a work branch first: git checkout -b ralph/your-work"
        exit 1
    fi
    export WORK_SCOPE="$WORK_DESCRIPTION"
fi

# Initialize circuit breaker
if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
    cb_init
    log_info "Circuit breaker initialized (cost limit: \$${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999})"
fi

# =============================================================================
# MAIN LOOP
# =============================================================================

print_banner

CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
ITERATION=0
START_TIME=$(date +%s)

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
log_info "Mode:       ${YELLOW}$MODE${NC}"
log_info "Branch:     ${CYAN}$CURRENT_BRANCH${NC}"
log_info "Prompt:     $PROMPT_FILE"
log_info "Model:      $MODEL"
[[ $MAX_ITERATIONS -gt 0 ]] && log_info "Max:        $MAX_ITERATIONS iterations"
[[ -n "$WORK_DESCRIPTION" ]] && log_info "Work:       $WORK_DESCRIPTION"
log_info "Circuit:    $([[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]] && echo "ENABLED" || echo "DISABLED")"
log_info "Auto-stop:  $([[ "$STOP_ON_COMPLETE" == "true" ]] && echo "on completion signal" || echo "manual only")"
log_info "Output:     $([[ "$PRETTY_OUTPUT" == "true" && -x "$FORMAT_OUTPUT_SCRIPT" ]] && echo "formatted" || echo "raw JSON")"
log_info "Timeouts:   Bash=${BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS}ms API=${API_TIMEOUT_MS}ms"
if [[ "$NO_PUSH" == "true" ]]; then
    log_warn "NO_PUSH=true: Work is LOCAL ONLY. Run 'git push' manually or set NO_PUSH=false"
fi
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
echo ""

# Trap for cleanup
trap 'echo ""; log_warn "Loop interrupted by user"; cleanup; exit 130' INT TERM

while true; do
    # Check iteration limit
    if [[ $MAX_ITERATIONS -gt 0 && $ITERATION -ge $MAX_ITERATIONS ]]; then
        log_success "Reached max iterations: $MAX_ITERATIONS"
        
        if [[ "$MODE" == "plan-work" ]]; then
            echo ""
            echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
            log_success "Scoped plan created for: $WORK_DESCRIPTION"
            log_info "To build, run: ./loop.sh 50"
            echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
        fi
        break
    fi

    # Check rate limit
    if ! check_rate_limit; then
        log_error "Rate limit exceeded. Stopping."
        break
    fi

    # Check circuit breaker
    if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
        CB_RESULT=$(cb_check_circuit 2>&1) || {
            log_error "Circuit breaker OPEN: $CB_RESULT"
            echo ""
            cb_status
            echo ""
            log_info "To reset: ./circuit-breaker.sh reset"
            break
        }
    fi

    ITERATION=$((ITERATION + 1))
    ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - START_TIME))
    OUTPUT_FILE="$OUTPUT_DIR/iteration-$ITERATION.json"
    
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}  ITERATION $ITERATION  │  Elapsed: ${ELAPSED}s  │  Branch: $CURRENT_BRANCH${NC}"
    if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
        cb_state=$(cb_get_state 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')
        cb_cost=$(echo "$cb_state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost // 0' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
        echo -e "${BLUE}  Est. Cost: \$${cb_cost}  │  Limit: \$${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999}${NC}"
    fi
    echo -e "${BLUE}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo ""

    # ==========================================================================
    # CORE RALPH LOOP - Clean Context on Each Iteration
    # ==========================================================================

    # Run pre-iteration hook (informational warnings)
    if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/hooks/pre-iteration.sh" ]]; then
        source "$SCRIPT_DIR/hooks/pre-iteration.sh" || true
    fi

    ITER_START=$(date +%s)

    # Run Claude with retry logic
    run_claude_with_retry "$PROMPT_FILE" "$OUTPUT_FILE" "$MODE"

    ITER_END=$(date +%s)
    ITER_DURATION=$((ITER_END - ITER_START))
    
    # Record call for rate limiting
    increment_call_count

    # Record iteration for circuit breaker
    if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
        cb_record_iteration
    fi

    # Check for errors in output
    if ERROR_MSG=$(check_for_errors "$OUTPUT_FILE"); then
        log_warn "Error detected: $ERROR_MSG"
        if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
            cb_record_error "$ERROR_MSG"
        fi
    fi

    # Check for completion signals
    if [[ "$STOP_ON_COMPLETE" == "true" ]]; then
        if SIGNAL=$(check_completion_signal "$OUTPUT_FILE"); then
            log_success "Completion signal detected: $SIGNAL"
            
            # Push final changes before exit
            if [[ "$NO_PUSH" == "false" ]]; then
                CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
                push_changes "$CURRENT_BRANCH" || log_warn "Final push failed"
            fi
            
            break
        fi
    fi

    # Push changes
    if [[ "$NO_PUSH" == "false" ]]; then
        CURRENT_BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
        if ! push_changes "$CURRENT_BRANCH"; then
            log_error "CRITICAL: Cannot push to remote after 3 retries."
            log_error "Work is only local! Stopping to prevent data loss."
            log_info "Fix credentials and run: git push origin $CURRENT_BRANCH"
            break
        fi
    fi

    log_success "Iteration $ITERATION completed (${ITER_DURATION}s)"
done

# =============================================================================
# SUMMARY
# =============================================================================

cleanup

TOTAL_TIME=$(($(date +%s) - START_TIME))
echo ""
echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
log_success "Ralph loop completed!"
log_info "Total iterations: $ITERATION"
log_info "Total time: ${TOTAL_TIME}s"
log_info "Avg time/iteration: $((TOTAL_TIME / (ITERATION > 0 ? ITERATION : 1)))s"

if [[ "$CB_ENABLED" == "true" ]]; then
    echo ""
    cb_status
fi

echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# Ralph Wiggum Setup Script for MMS v2.1
# =============================================================================
# This script prepares the MMS project to use the Ralph Wiggum methodology.
#
# Improvements v2.1:
# - All scripts made executable
# - jq installation check
# - Claude CLI settings.json configuration guidance
# - Timeout configuration guidance
#
# Usage:
#   chmod +x setup.sh
#   ./setup.sh
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

log_info() { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
log_success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"; }
log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
log_error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"; }

echo ""
echo -e "${BLUE}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}║     Ralph Wiggum Setup for MMS v2.1                              ║${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}║     Original Huntley Method - External Loop, Clean Context       ║${NC}"
echo -e "${BLUE}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
echo ""

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"

# =============================================================================
# 1. Check prerequisites
# =============================================================================
log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

MISSING_CRITICAL=false
MISSING_OPTIONAL=()

# Claude CLI
if command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Claude CLI found: $(claude --version 2>/dev/null || echo 'version unknown')"
else
    log_error "Claude CLI not found. Install with: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-cli"
    MISSING_CRITICAL=true
fi

# Bun
if command -v bun &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Bun found: $(bun --version)"
else
    log_warn "Bun not found. Install with: curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("bun")
fi

# Docker
if command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Docker found: $(docker --version)"
else
    log_warn "Docker not found. Sandbox execution will not be available."
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("docker")
fi

# Git
if git rev-parse --git-dir &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "Git repository detected"
else
    log_warn "Not in a git repository. Ralph requires git for state persistence."
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("git-repo")
fi

# jq (required for circuit breaker)
if command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "jq found: $(jq --version)"
else
    log_warn "jq not found - circuit breaker features will be limited"
    log_info "Install with: apt install jq (Linux) or brew install jq (macOS)"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("jq")
fi

# envsubst (required for plan-work mode)
if command -v envsubst &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "envsubst found"
else
    log_warn "envsubst not found - plan-work mode may not work"
    log_info "Install with: apt install gettext-base (Linux) or brew install gettext (macOS)"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("envsubst")
fi

# bc (optional, used for floating point math)
if command -v bc &> /dev/null; then
    log_success "bc found"
else
    log_warn "bc not found - using awk fallback for floating point math"
    MISSING_OPTIONAL+=("bc")
fi

if [[ "$MISSING_CRITICAL" == "true" ]]; then
    log_error "Critical dependencies missing. Please install them and re-run setup."
    exit 1
fi

# =============================================================================
# 2. Create directory structure
# =============================================================================
log_info "Creating directory structure..."

mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/specs"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/lib"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/services"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/api"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/workers"
mkdir -p "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/optimizer"

log_success "Directory structure created"

# =============================================================================
# 3. Make ALL scripts executable
# =============================================================================
log_info "Making scripts executable..."

chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/loop.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/monitor.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
chmod +x "$SCRIPT_DIR/setup.sh" 2>/dev/null || true

log_success "All scripts are executable"

# =============================================================================
# 5. Copy specs from project (if they exist)
# =============================================================================
log_info "Checking for existing specs..."

if [[ -d "/mnt/project" ]]; then
    log_info "Found project specs. Copying to specs/..."
    
    # Map existing spec files
    declare -A spec_files=(
        ["MMS_SPEC_001_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md"]="001-executive-summary.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_002_ARCHITECTURE.md"]="002-architecture.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_003_DATABASE_SCHEMA.md"]="003-database-schema.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_004_DOMAIN_SERVICES.md"]="004-domain-services.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_005_API.md"]="005-api.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_006_USER_INTERFACE.md"]="006-user-interface.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_007_INTEGRATION.md"]="007-integration.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_008_OPERATIONS.md"]="008-operations.md"
        ["MMS_SPEC_009_DEMO_APPLICATION.md"]="009-demo-application.md"
    )
    
    for src in "${!spec_files[@]}"; do
        if [[ -f "/mnt/project/$src" ]]; then
            dst="${spec_files[$src]}"
            cp "/mnt/project/$src" "$PROJECT_ROOT/specs/$dst"
            log_success "Copied $src → specs/$dst"
        fi
    done
else
    log_warn "No existing project specs found at /mnt/project"
    log_info "You'll need to create specs manually in the specs/ directory"
fi

# =============================================================================
# 6. Create .gitignore entries for Ralph
# =============================================================================
log_info "Updating .gitignore..."

GITIGNORE_ENTRIES="
# Ralph Wiggum
/ralph/.ralph-state/
/tmp/ralph-*

# Docker
docker/ralph-sandbox/postgres_data/
docker/ralph-sandbox/redis_data/
docker/ralph-sandbox/minio_data/
"

if [[ -f "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore" ]]; then
    if ! grep -q "Ralph Wiggum" "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "$GITIGNORE_ENTRIES" >> "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore"
        log_success ".gitignore updated"
    else
        log_info ".gitignore already contains Ralph entries"
    fi
else
    echo "$GITIGNORE_ENTRIES" > "$PROJECT_ROOT/.gitignore"
    log_success ".gitignore created"
fi

# =============================================================================
# 7. Claude CLI Settings Configuration (IMPORTANT!)
# =============================================================================
echo ""
echo -e "${YELLOW}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}  IMPORTANT: Claude CLI Timeout Configuration${NC}"
echo -e "${YELLOW}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}"
echo ""
echo "Claude CLI has a default 2-minute timeout on bash commands."
echo "Long-running builds, tests, and optimizer runs will be KILLED!"
echo ""
echo "To configure timeouts, add this to .claude/settings.json in the project root:"
echo ""
echo -e "${CYAN}{"
echo '  "env": {'
echo '    "BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS": "1800000",'
echo '    "BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS": "7200000",'
echo '    "API_TIMEOUT_MS": "600000"'
echo '  }'
echo -e "}${NC}"
echo ""
echo "Alternatively, loop.sh will export these as environment variables."
echo ""

# Create project-level settings file if it doesn't exist
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/.claude"
CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE="$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR/settings.json"

if [[ ! -f "$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE" ]]; then
    log_info "Creating project-level Claude settings file..."
    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_DIR"
    cat > "$CLAUDE_SETTINGS_FILE" << 'EOF'
{
  "env": {
    "BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS": "1800000",
    "BASH_MAX_TIMEOUT_MS": "7200000",
    "API_TIMEOUT_MS": "600000",
    "MCP_TIMEOUT": "30000",
    "CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC": "1"
  }
}
EOF
    log_success "Created .claude/settings.json with timeout configuration"
else
    log_info ".claude/settings.json already exists"
    log_info "Please verify it contains the timeout configuration above"
fi

# =============================================================================
# 8. Summary
# =============================================================================
echo ""
echo -e "${GREEN}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗${NC}"
echo -e "${GREEN}║     Setup Complete!                                              ║${NC}"
echo -e "${GREEN}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝${NC}"
echo ""

if [[ ${#MISSING_OPTIONAL[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Optional dependencies missing:${NC} ${MISSING_OPTIONAL[*]}"
    echo "Some features may be limited. Install them for full functionality."
    echo ""
fi

echo "Next steps:"
echo ""
echo "  1. Verify Claude CLI timeout settings:"
echo "     cat .claude/settings.json"
echo ""
echo "  2. Start with planning mode:"
echo "     cd ralph"
echo "     ./loop.sh plan"
echo ""
echo "  3. Then build:"
echo "     ./loop.sh 50"
echo ""
echo "  For sandbox execution (recommended):"
echo "     cd docker/ralph-sandbox"
echo "     docker-compose up -d"
echo "     docker exec -it ralph-sandbox bash"
echo "     cd /home/ralph/workspace/ralph"
echo "     ./loop.sh"
echo ""
echo "Documentation: ralph/README.md"
echo ""

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# Ralph Wiggum Monitor - Real-Time Observation v2.1
# =============================================================================
# Monitors Ralph loop progress in a separate window.
#
# v2.1 Improvements:
# - Cross-platform date handling
# - Better error handling
# - Timeout status display
#
# Usage:
#   ./monitor.sh           # Dashboard mode (tmux)
#   ./monitor.sh stats     # Stats only
#   ./monitor.sh tail      # Follow logs
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

# =============================================================================
# UTILITY FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

# Cross-platform date from timestamp
date_from_timestamp() {
    local timestamp="$1"
    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU date (Linux)
        date -d "@$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    else
        # BSD date (macOS)
        date -r "$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    fi
}

# Cross-platform watch alternative
portable_watch() {
    local interval="$1"
    shift
    local cmd="$*"
    while true; do
        clear
        echo -e "${CYAN}Every ${interval}s: ${cmd}${NC}"
        echo ""
        eval "$cmd"
        sleep "$interval"
    done
}

# =============================================================================
# FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

show_stats() {
    echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo -e "${CYAN}  Ralph Wiggum Monitor v2.1 - MMS Project${NC}"
    echo -e "${CYAN}═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    # Git stats
    echo -e "${BLUE}Git Status:${NC}"
    echo "  Branch:        $(git branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo 'N/A')"
    echo "  Commits today: $(git log --since='00:00' --oneline 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ' || echo '0')"
    echo "  Last commit:   $(git log -1 --format='%h %s' 2>/dev/null || echo 'N/A')"
    echo ""
    
    # Timeout configuration
    echo -e "${BLUE}Timeout Configuration:${NC}"
    if [[ -n "${BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS:-}" ]]; then
        echo "  Bash timeout:  ${BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS}ms ($(( BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS / 60000 )) min)"
    else
        echo "  Bash timeout:  ${YELLOW}NOT SET (using 2min default!)${NC}"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${API_TIMEOUT_MS:-}" ]]; then
        echo "  API timeout:   ${API_TIMEOUT_MS}ms ($(( API_TIMEOUT_MS / 60000 )) min)"
    else
        echo "  API timeout:   ${YELLOW}NOT SET${NC}"
    fi
    echo ""
    
    # Implementation plan stats
    if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" ]]; then
        local total done
        total=$(grep -c "^\- \[" "$SCRIPT_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" 2>/dev/null) || total=0
        done=$(grep -c "^\- \[x\]" "$SCRIPT_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" 2>/dev/null) || done=0
        local pending=$((total - done))
        local percent=0
        [[ $total -gt 0 ]] && percent=$((done * 100 / total))
        
        echo -e "${BLUE}Implementation Plan:${NC}"
        echo "  Total tasks:   $total"
        echo -e "  Completed:     ${GREEN}$done${NC}"
        echo -e "  Pending:       ${YELLOW}$pending${NC}"
        echo "  Progress:      $percent%"
        echo ""
    fi
    
    # Rate limiting
    local calls_file="/tmp/ralph-calls-$(date +%Y%m%d%H)"
    local calls=0
    [[ -f "$calls_file" ]] && calls=$(cat "$calls_file")
    echo -e "${BLUE}Rate Limiting:${NC}"
    echo "  Calls this hour: $calls / ${MAX_CALLS_PER_HOUR:-9999999}"
    echo "  Reset in:        $((60 - $(date +%M))) minutes"
    echo ""
    
    # Circuit breaker status
    if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh" ]]; then
        source "$SCRIPT_DIR/circuit-breaker.sh"
        echo -e "${BLUE}Circuit Breaker:${NC}"
        if command -v jq &> /dev/null && [[ -f "/tmp/ralph-circuit-breaker/state.json" ]]; then
            local state
            state=$(cat /tmp/ralph-circuit-breaker/state.json 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')
            local iterations
            iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations // 0' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            local cost
            cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost // 0' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            local no_progress
            no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count // 0' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            
            echo "  Iterations:    $iterations"
            printf "  Est. cost:     \$%.2f / \$%d\n" "$cost" "${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999}"
            echo "  No progress:   $no_progress / ${CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD:-9999999}"
            
            if cb_check_circuit > /dev/null 2>&1; then
                echo -e "  Status:        ${GREEN}CLOSED (OK)${NC}"
            else
                echo -e "  Status:        ${RED}OPEN (BLOCKED)${NC}"
            fi
        else
            echo "  Status:        Not initialized or jq missing"
        fi
        echo ""
    fi
    
    # Docker status (if available)
    if command -v docker &> /dev/null; then
        echo -e "${BLUE}Docker Services:${NC}"
        docker ps --format "  {{.Names}}: {{.Status}}" 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo "  Docker not running"
        echo ""
    fi
    
    # Recent activity from IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
    echo -e "${BLUE}Recent Completed Tasks:${NC}"
    grep "^\- \[x\]" "$SCRIPT_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" 2>/dev/null | tail -5 | sed 's/^/  /' || echo "  None yet"
    echo ""
    
    # Next pending tasks
    echo -e "${BLUE}Next Pending Tasks:${NC}"
    grep "^\- \[ \].*P1:" "$SCRIPT_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md" 2>/dev/null | head -5 | sed 's/^/  /' || echo "  No P1 tasks found"
    echo ""
}

tail_logs() {
    echo -e "${CYAN}Following git log (Ctrl+C to stop)...${NC}"
    echo ""
    if command -v watch &> /dev/null; then
        watch -n 5 "git log --oneline -20 2>/dev/null"
    else
        portable_watch 5 "git log --oneline -20 2>/dev/null"
    fi
}

dashboard() {
    # Check if tmux is available
    if ! command -v tmux &> /dev/null; then
        echo "tmux not found. Install with: brew install tmux"
        echo "Falling back to stats mode..."
        while true; do
            clear
            show_stats
            sleep 10
        done
        return
    fi

    # Determine watch command (native or portable fallback)
    local watch_cmd
    if command -v watch &> /dev/null; then
        watch_cmd="watch -n"
    else
        watch_cmd="portable_watch"
    fi

    # Create tmux session with dashboard layout
    local session="ralph-monitor"

    tmux kill-session -t "$session" 2>/dev/null || true

    tmux new-session -d -s "$session" -n "monitor"

    # Pane 0: Stats (updates every 10s)
    tmux send-keys -t "$session:0" "while true; do clear; $0 stats; sleep 10; done" Enter

    # Pane 1: Git log
    tmux split-window -t "$session:0" -h
    tmux send-keys -t "$session:0.1" "while true; do clear; git log --oneline -15; sleep 5; done" Enter

    # Pane 2: Implementation plan tail
    tmux split-window -t "$session:0.0" -v
    tmux send-keys -t "$session:0.2" "while true; do clear; head -50 $SCRIPT_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md 2>/dev/null || echo 'No plan yet'; sleep 5; done" Enter

    # Pane 3: Docker logs (if available)
    tmux split-window -t "$session:0.1" -v
    tmux send-keys -t "$session:0.3" "docker logs -f ralph-sandbox 2>/dev/null || echo 'Waiting for container...'; sleep 5" Enter

    # Attach to session
    tmux attach -t "$session"
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN
# =============================================================================

case "${1:-dashboard}" in
    stats)
        show_stats
        ;;
    tail)
        tail_logs
        ;;
    dashboard)
        dashboard
        ;;
    *)
        echo "Ralph Wiggum Monitor v2.1"
        echo ""
        echo "Usage: $0 [stats|tail|dashboard]"
        echo ""
        echo "Commands:"
        echo "  stats      Show current statistics (default for non-tmux)"
        echo "  tail       Follow git log"
        echo "  dashboard  Full tmux dashboard (default)"
        exit 1
        ;;
esac

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# Ralph Wiggum Circuit Breaker - Production Version 2.1
# =============================================================================
# Prevents runaway loops and excessive API costs.
#
# Improvements v2.1:
# - bc fallback to awk for systems without bc
# - Cross-platform date compatibility (Linux/macOS)
# - Improved error handling
# - Better jq fallback
#
# Usage (standalone):
#   ./circuit-breaker.sh status    # Show current state
#   ./circuit-breaker.sh reset     # Reset all state
#   ./circuit-breaker.sh check     # Check if circuit is open (exit 1 = open)
#
# Usage (sourced by loop.sh):
#   source circuit-breaker.sh
#   cb_init
#   cb_check_circuit || exit 1
#   cb_record_iteration
#   cb_record_error "error message"
#
# The circuit opens when:
# - No progress (git commits) for N iterations
# - Same error repeated N times
# - Estimated cost exceeds limit
# =============================================================================

# Configuration (can be overridden via environment)
CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD=${CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD:-9999999}
CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD=${CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD:-5}
CB_MAX_COST_USD=${CB_MAX_COST_USD:-9999999}
CB_STATE_DIR="${CB_STATE_DIR:-/tmp/ralph-circuit-breaker}"
CB_STATE_FILE="${CB_STATE_DIR}/state.json"

# Cost estimate per iteration (Opus model)
# Input: ~5k tokens ($0.015/1k = $0.075)
# Output: ~10k tokens ($0.075/1k = $0.75)
# Subagents: ~$0.10 average
# Total: ~$0.40-0.50 per iteration
COST_PER_ITERATION=${COST_PER_ITERATION:-0.45}

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m'

# =============================================================================
# UTILITY FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

# Safe floating point addition (bc or awk fallback)
safe_add() {
    local a="$1"
    local b="$2"
    if command -v bc &> /dev/null; then
        echo "$a + $b" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.4f\", $a + $b}"
    else
        awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.4f\", $a + $b}"
    fi
}

# Safe floating point comparison (bc or awk fallback)
safe_gte() {
    local a="$1"
    local b="$2"
    if command -v bc &> /dev/null; then
        [[ $(echo "$a >= $b" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo 0) -eq 1 ]]
    else
        awk "BEGIN {exit !($a >= $b)}"
    fi
}

# Cross-platform date from timestamp
date_from_timestamp() {
    local timestamp="$1"
    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU date (Linux)
        date -d "@$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    else
        # BSD date (macOS)
        date -r "$timestamp" '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S' 2>/dev/null || echo "N/A"
    fi
}

# Cross-platform ISO date
iso_date() {
    if date --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU date
        date -Iseconds 2>/dev/null || date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z'
    else
        # BSD date (macOS)
        date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z'
    fi
}

# Check if jq is available
has_jq() {
    command -v jq &> /dev/null
}

# =============================================================================
# CORE FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

cb_init() {
    mkdir -p "$CB_STATE_DIR"
    if [[ ! -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]]; then
        local start_time
        start_time=$(date +%s)
        
        if has_jq; then
            echo '{
                "iterations": 0,
                "last_commit": "",
                "no_progress_count": 0,
                "errors": [],
                "estimated_cost": 0,
                "start_time": '"$start_time"',
                "session_id": "'$$'"
            }' | jq '.' > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
        else
            # Fallback without jq
            cat > "$CB_STATE_FILE" << EOF
{
    "iterations": 0,
    "last_commit": "",
    "no_progress_count": 0,
    "errors": [],
    "estimated_cost": 0,
    "start_time": $start_time,
    "session_id": "$$"
}
EOF
        fi
    fi
}

cb_get_state() {
    if [[ ! -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]]; then
        cb_init
    fi
    cat "$CB_STATE_FILE"
}

cb_update_state() {
    local state="$1"
    if has_jq; then
        echo "$state" | jq '.' > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
    else
        echo "$state" > "$CB_STATE_FILE"
    fi
}

cb_record_iteration() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - circuit breaker recording limited"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local current_commit
    current_commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "none")
    local last_commit
    last_commit=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.last_commit')
    local iterations
    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
    local no_progress
    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
    local cost
    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
    
    iterations=$((iterations + 1))
    cost=$(safe_add "$cost" "$COST_PER_ITERATION")
    
    if [[ "$current_commit" == "$last_commit" ]]; then
        no_progress=$((no_progress + 1))
    else
        no_progress=0
    fi
    
    state=$(echo "$state" | jq \
        --arg commit "$current_commit" \
        --argjson iter "$iterations" \
        --argjson np "$no_progress" \
        --arg cost "$cost" \
        '.last_commit = $commit | .iterations = $iter | .no_progress_count = $np | .estimated_cost = ($cost | tonumber)')
    
    cb_update_state "$state"
}

cb_record_error() {
    local error_msg="$1"
    
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - error not recorded"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(iso_date)
    
    # Create error object with timestamp
    state=$(echo "$state" | jq \
        --arg err "$error_msg" \
        --arg ts "$timestamp" \
        '.errors += [{"message": $err, "timestamp": $ts}] | .errors = .errors[-10:]')
    
    cb_update_state "$state"
}

cb_check_circuit() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        # Without jq, we can't properly check the circuit
        # Fail open (allow execution) but warn
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - circuit check skipped" >&2
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local no_progress
    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
    local cost
    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
    local iterations
    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
    
    # Check no progress
    if [[ $no_progress -ge $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD ]]; then
        echo "No progress for $no_progress iterations (threshold: $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD)"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check cost limit
    if safe_gte "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"; then
        printf "Estimated cost \$%.2f exceeds limit \$%d\n" "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check repeated errors (same error message 5+ times in last 10)
    local error_messages
    error_messages=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '[.errors[].message] | group_by(.) | map({message: .[0], count: length}) | sort_by(-.count) | .[0]')
    local top_error_count
    top_error_count=$(echo "$error_messages" | jq -r '.count // 0')
    local top_error_msg
    top_error_msg=$(echo "$error_messages" | jq -r '.message // "none"')
    
    if [[ $top_error_count -ge $CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD ]]; then
        echo "Same error repeated $top_error_count times: $top_error_msg"
        return 1
    fi
    
    return 0
}

cb_status() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${CYAN}Circuit Breaker Status:${NC}"
        echo "  jq not installed - detailed status unavailable"
        echo "  State file: $CB_STATE_FILE"
        [[ -f "$CB_STATE_FILE" ]] && echo "  Raw state: $(cat "$CB_STATE_FILE" | head -c 200)..."
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    local iterations
    iterations=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.iterations')
    local no_progress
    no_progress=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.no_progress_count')
    local cost
    cost=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.estimated_cost')
    local error_count
    error_count=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.errors | length')
    local start_time
    start_time=$(echo "$state" | jq -r '.start_time')
    local elapsed
    elapsed=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
    
    echo -e "${CYAN}Circuit Breaker Status:${NC}"
    echo "  Session start:   $(date_from_timestamp "$start_time")"
    echo "  Elapsed:         ${elapsed}s"
    echo "  Iterations:      $iterations"
    echo "  No progress:     $no_progress / $CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD"
    printf "  Estimated cost:  \$%.2f / \$%d\n" "$cost" "$CB_MAX_COST_USD"
    echo "  Recent errors:   $error_count"
    
    if cb_check_circuit > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo -e "  Status:          ${GREEN}CLOSED (OK)${NC}"
    else
        echo -e "  Status:          ${RED}OPEN (BLOCKED)${NC}"
        echo ""
        echo -e "${YELLOW}Reason:${NC}"
        cb_check_circuit 2>&1 | sed 's/^/  /'
    fi
}

cb_reset() {
    rm -f "$CB_STATE_FILE"
    cb_init
    echo -e "${GREEN}Circuit breaker reset${NC}"
}

cb_show_errors() {
    if ! has_jq; then
        echo -e "${CYAN}Recent Errors:${NC}"
        echo "  jq not installed - cannot show errors"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local state
    state=$(cb_get_state)
    echo -e "${CYAN}Recent Errors:${NC}"
    echo "$state" | jq -r '.errors[] | "  [\(.timestamp)] \(.message)"' 2>/dev/null || echo "  (none)"
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN (when executed directly)
# =============================================================================

if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    case "${1:-status}" in
        init)
            cb_init
            echo "Circuit breaker initialized"
            ;;
        record)
            cb_record_iteration
            echo "Iteration recorded"
            ;;
        error)
            if [[ -z "$2" ]]; then
                echo "Usage: $0 error \"error message\""
                exit 1
            fi
            cb_record_error "$2"
            echo "Error recorded"
            ;;
        check)
            if cb_check_circuit; then
                echo -e "${GREEN}Circuit CLOSED (OK to proceed)${NC}"
                exit 0
            else
                echo -e "${RED}Circuit OPEN (blocked)${NC}"
                exit 1
            fi
            ;;
        status)
            cb_status
            ;;
        errors)
            cb_show_errors
            ;;
        reset)
            cb_reset
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Ralph Wiggum Circuit Breaker v2.1"
            echo ""
            echo "Usage: $0 [command]"
            echo ""
            echo "Commands:"
            echo "  status    Show current state (default)"
            echo "  check     Check if circuit is open (exit 1 = open)"
            echo "  reset     Reset all state"
            echo "  errors    Show recent errors"
            echo "  init      Initialize state file"
            echo "  record    Record an iteration"
            echo "  error MSG Record an error message"
            echo ""
            echo "Environment Variables:"
            echo "  CB_NO_PROGRESS_THRESHOLD  Iterations without commit (default: 10)"
            echo "  CB_SAME_ERROR_THRESHOLD   Repeated error count (default: 5)"
            echo "  CB_MAX_COST_USD           Cost limit in USD (default: 9999999 - unlimited for 24h+ runs)"
            echo "  COST_PER_ITERATION        Est. cost per iteration (default: 0.45)"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
fi

   257→    [[ "${line:0:1}" != "{" ]] && { echo "$line"; return; }

   259→    # Single jq call to extract type and validate
   260→    local extracted
   261→    extracted=$(echo "$line" | jq -c "$JQ_EXTRACT_FILTER" 2>/dev/null) || {
   262→        echo "$line"  # Not valid JSON, pass through
   263→        return
   264→    }
   265→
   266→    local msg_type content tool_error
   267→    msg_type=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')
   268→    content=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -c '.content // []')
   269→    tool_error=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -r '.tool_error // empty')
   270→
   271→    # Handle tool result errors (from top-level tool_use_result field)
   272→    local tool_error_handled=false
   273→    if [[ -n "$tool_error" ]]; then
   274→        process_error "$tool_error"
   275→        tool_error_handled=true
   276→    fi
   277→
   278→    case "$msg_type" in
   279→        assistant)
   280→            # Process content blocks
   281→            local block_count
   282→            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length')
   283→
   284→            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
   285→                local block block_type
   286→                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]")
   287→                block_type=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')
   288→
   289→                case "$block_type" in
   290→                    text)
   291→                        local text
   292→                        text=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.text // ""')
   293→                        process_text "$text"
   294→                        ;;
   295→                    tool_use)
   296→                        local tool_name tool_input
   297→                        tool_name=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.name // "unknown"')
   298→                        tool_input=$(echo "$block" | jq -c '.input // {}')
   299→                        process_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
   300→                        ;;
   301→                    thinking)
   302→                        local thinking
   303→                        thinking=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.thinking // ""')
   304→                        process_thinking "$thinking"
   305→                        ;;
   306→                esac
   307→            done
   308→            ;;
   309→
   310→        user)
   311→            # Check for tool result errors in user messages
   312→            # Skip if already handled via tool_use_result field
   313→            [[ "$tool_error_handled" == "true" ]] && return
   314→
   315→            if [[ "$content" == "null" || -z "$content" ]]; then
   316→                return
   317→            fi
   318→
   319→            local block_count
   320→            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length' 2>/dev/null) || return
   321→
   322→            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
   323→                local block block_type is_error
   324→                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]" 2>/dev/null) || continue
   325→                block_type=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.type // ""')
   326→
   327→                if [[ "$block_type" == "tool_result" ]]; then
   328→                    is_error=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
   329→                    if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
   330→                        local error_content
   331→                        error_content=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.content // "Tool error"' | head -n 1)
   332→                        process_error "$error_content"
   333→                    fi
   334→                fi
   335→            done
   336→            ;;
   337→
   338→        error)
   339→            local error_msg
   340→            error_msg=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.error.message // .error // .message // "Unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
   341→            process_error "$error_msg"
   342→            ;;
   343→
   344→        system|result)
   345→            # System messages - show if they have meaningful content
   346→            local result_text
   347→            result_text=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.result // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null)
   348→            [[ -n "$result_text" ]] && process_text "$result_text"
   349→            ;;
   350→
   351→        # Skip internal/lifecycle events silently
   352→        message_start|message_delta|message_stop|content_block_start|content_block_delta|content_block_stop)
   353→            ;;
   354→
   355→        *)
   356→            # Unknown type - skip silently unless debug
   357→            ;;
   358→    esac
   359→
   360→    # Periodic summary
   361→    check_summary_needed
   362→}
   363→
   364→# =============================================================================
   365→# MAIN
   366→# =============================================================================
   367→
   368→main() {
   369→    # Check for jq
   370→    if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
   371→        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - passing through raw output" >&2
   372→        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} Install: brew install jq (macOS) / apt install jq (Linux)" >&2
   373→        cat
   374→        return 0
   375→    fi
   376→
   377→    # Trap for clean exit with summary
   378→    trap 'echo ""; print_summary; exit 130' INT TERM
   379→
   380→    # Process stdin line by line
   381→    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
   382→        process_line "$line"
   383→    done
   384→
   385→    # Final summary
   386→    print_summary
   387→}
   388→
   389→# Run if executed directly (not sourced)
   390→if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
   391→    main "$@"
   392→fi
   393→

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# format-output.sh - Human-readable output formatting for Ralph loop monitoring
# =============================================================================
# Transforms Claude CLI stream-json output into readable format.
#
# Usage:
#   claude -p ... --output-format=stream-json | ./format-output.sh
#   cat claude-output.json | ./format-output.sh
#
# Performance: Optimized for streaming - single jq call per line, cached timestamps
# =============================================================================

set -uo pipefail
# Note: -e intentionally omitted - parser errors should not kill the formatter

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
DIM='\033[2m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'

# Icons
ICON_TEXT="T"
ICON_TOOL=">"
ICON_THINK="."
ICON_TODO="L"
ICON_ERROR="E"
ICON_COMMIT="C"

# Configuration
MAX_LINE_WIDTH=${MAX_LINE_WIDTH:-120}
SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC=${SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC:-300}
RAW_OUTPUT_FILE=${RAW_OUTPUT_FILE:-}
SHOW_THINKING=${SHOW_THINKING:-false}

# State tracking
START_TIME=$(date +%s)
LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$START_TIME
LINE_COUNT=0
ERROR_COUNT=0
TOOL_COUNT=0
TEXT_COUNT=0
COMMIT_COUNT=0
TODO_COMPLETED=0
TODO_PENDING=0
TODO_IN_PROGRESS=0

# Timestamp cache (avoid calling date on every line)
LAST_TS_SEC=0
CACHED_TS=""

# =============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

timestamp() {
    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    if [[ $now -ne $LAST_TS_SEC ]]; then
        CACHED_TS=$(date '+[%H:%M]')
        LAST_TS_SEC=$now
    fi
    printf '%s' "$CACHED_TS"
}

truncate_line() {
    local text="$1"
    local max_len="${2:-$MAX_LINE_WIDTH}"
    local len=${#text}

    if [[ $len -gt $max_len ]]; then
        # UTF-8 safe truncation using parameter expansion with fallback
        printf '%s...' "${text:0:$((max_len - 3))}"
    else
        printf '%s' "$text"
    fi
}

format_elapsed() {
    local seconds="$1"
    local hours=$((seconds / 3600))
    local minutes=$(((seconds % 3600) / 60))
    local secs=$((seconds % 60))

    if [[ $hours -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf "%dh%02dm" "$hours" "$minutes"
    elif [[ $minutes -gt 0 ]]; then
        printf "%dm%02ds" "$minutes" "$secs"
    else
        printf "%ds" "$secs"
    fi
}

print_summary() {
    local now elapsed
    now=$(date +%s)
    elapsed=$((now - START_TIME))

    echo ""
    echo -e "${BLUE}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE} $(format_elapsed "$elapsed") | Tools: $TOOL_COUNT | Text: $TEXT_COUNT | Errors: $ERROR_COUNT${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE} Commits: $COMMIT_COUNT | Todos: $TODO_IN_PROGRESS active, $TODO_COMPLETED done, $TODO_PENDING pending${NC}"
    echo -e "${BLUE}════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
    echo ""

    LAST_SUMMARY_TIME=$now
}

check_summary_needed() {
    local now
    now=$(date +%s)
    if [[ $((now - LAST_SUMMARY_TIME)) -ge $SUMMARY_INTERVAL_SEC ]]; then
        print_summary
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# JQ FILTER - Extract all needed fields in one call
# =============================================================================

# This jq filter extracts everything we need from a line in a single pass
# Note: tool_use_result can be a string (error msg) or object, handle both
JQ_EXTRACT_FILTER='{
  type: .type,
  content: (if .message.content then .message.content else null end),
  tool_error: (
    if (.tool_use_result | type) == "object" and .tool_use_result.is_error
    then .tool_use_result.content
    elif (.tool_use_result | type) == "string" and (.tool_use_result | startswith("Error"))
    then .tool_use_result
    else null
    end
  )
}'

# =============================================================================
# MESSAGE PROCESSORS
# =============================================================================

process_text() {
    local text="$1"
    [[ -z "${text// /}" ]] && return

    TEXT_COUNT=$((TEXT_COUNT + 1))

    # Check for commit-related messages
    if [[ "$text" =~ (committed|commit[[:space:]]+[a-f0-9]{7,}|git[[:space:]]+commit|Co-Authored-By) ]]; then
        COMMIT_COUNT=$((COMMIT_COUNT + 1))
        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${GREEN}${ICON_COMMIT}${NC} ${GREEN}$(truncate_line "$text")${NC}"
    else
        echo -e "$(timestamp) ${ICON_TEXT} $(truncate_line "$text")"
    fi
}

process_tool_use() {
    local name="$1"
    local input_json="$2"

    TOOL_COUNT=$((TOOL_COUNT + 1))

    local description=""

    case "$name" in
        Read)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.file_path // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Read: ${description##*/}"  # basename only
            ;;
        Write)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.file_path // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Write: ${description##*/}"
            ;;
        Edit)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.file_path // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Edit: ${description##*/}"
            ;;
        Bash)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.description // (.command | .[0:50]) // "cmd"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="$ $description"
            ;;
        Glob)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.pattern // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Glob: $description"
            ;;
        Grep)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.pattern // "?"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Grep: $description"
            ;;
        Task)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.description // "task"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="Task: $description"
            ;;
        TodoWrite)
            # Extract todo counts - accumulate, don't overwrite
            local completed pending in_progress
            completed=$(echo "$input_json" | jq '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="completed")] | length' 2>/dev/null) || completed=0
            pending=$(echo "$input_json" | jq '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="pending")] | length' 2>/dev/null) || pending=0
            in_progress=$(echo "$input_json" | jq '[.todos[]? | select(.status=="in_progress")] | length' 2>/dev/null) || in_progress=0

            TODO_COMPLETED=$completed
            TODO_PENDING=$pending
            TODO_IN_PROGRESS=$in_progress

            echo -e "$(timestamp) ${YELLOW}${ICON_TODO}${NC} ${YELLOW}Todos: $in_progress active, $completed done, $pending pending${NC}"
            return
            ;;
        WebFetch|WebSearch)
            description=$(echo "$input_json" | jq -r '.url // .query // "web"' 2>/dev/null)
            description="$name: $description"
            ;;
        mcp__playwright__*)
            description="Playwright: ${name#mcp__playwright__}"
            ;;
        *)
            description="$name"
            ;;
    esac

    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${CYAN}${ICON_TOOL}${NC} ${CYAN}$(truncate_line "$description" 100)${NC}"
}

process_thinking() {
    [[ "$SHOW_THINKING" != "true" ]] && return

    local thinking="$1"
    local first_line
    first_line=$(echo "$thinking" | head -n 1)
    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${DIM}${ICON_THINK} $(truncate_line "$first_line" 80)${NC}"
}

process_error() {
    local error_msg="$1"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    echo -e "$(timestamp) ${RED}${ICON_ERROR} ERROR: $error_msg${NC}" >&2
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN LINE PROCESSOR
# =============================================================================

process_line() {
    local line="$1"

    LINE_COUNT=$((LINE_COUNT + 1))

    # Save raw output if configured
    [[ -n "$RAW_OUTPUT_FILE" ]] && echo "$line" >> "$RAW_OUTPUT_FILE"

    # Skip empty lines
    [[ -z "$line" ]] && return

    # Quick rejection of non-JSON (faster than jq validation)
    [[ "${line:0:1}" != "{" ]] && { echo "$line"; return; }

    # Single jq call to extract type and validate
    local extracted
    extracted=$(echo "$line" | jq -c "$JQ_EXTRACT_FILTER" 2>/dev/null) || {
        echo "$line"  # Not valid JSON, pass through
        return
    }

    local msg_type content tool_error
    msg_type=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')
    content=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -c '.content // []')
    tool_error=$(echo "$extracted" | jq -r '.tool_error // empty')

    # Handle tool result errors (from top-level tool_use_result field)
    local tool_error_handled=false
    if [[ -n "$tool_error" ]]; then
        process_error "$tool_error"
        tool_error_handled=true
    fi

    case "$msg_type" in
        assistant)
            # Process content blocks
            local block_count
            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length')

            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
                local block block_type
                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]")
                block_type=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')

                case "$block_type" in
                    text)
                        local text
                        text=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.text // ""')
                        process_text "$text"
                        ;;
                    tool_use)
                        local tool_name tool_input
                        tool_name=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.name // "unknown"')
                        tool_input=$(echo "$block" | jq -c '.input // {}')
                        process_tool_use "$tool_name" "$tool_input"
                        ;;
                    thinking)
                        local thinking
                        thinking=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.thinking // ""')
                        process_thinking "$thinking"
                        ;;
                esac
            done
            ;;

        user)
            # Check for tool result errors in user messages
            # Skip if already handled via tool_use_result field
            [[ "$tool_error_handled" == "true" ]] && return

            if [[ "$content" == "null" || -z "$content" ]]; then
                return
            fi

            local block_count
            block_count=$(echo "$content" | jq 'length' 2>/dev/null) || return

            for ((i=0; i<block_count; i++)); do
                local block block_type is_error
                block=$(echo "$content" | jq -c ".[$i]" 2>/dev/null) || continue
                block_type=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.type // ""')

                if [[ "$block_type" == "tool_result" ]]; then
                    is_error=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
                    if [[ "$is_error" == "true" ]]; then
                        local error_content
                        error_content=$(echo "$block" | jq -r '.content // "Tool error"' | head -n 1)
                        process_error "$error_content"
                    fi
                fi
            done
            ;;

        error)
            local error_msg
            error_msg=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.error.message // .error // .message // "Unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
            process_error "$error_msg"
            ;;

        system|result)
            # System messages - show if they have meaningful content
            local result_text
            result_text=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.result // .text // empty' 2>/dev/null)
            [[ -n "$result_text" ]] && process_text "$result_text"
            ;;

        # Skip internal/lifecycle events silently
        message_start|message_delta|message_stop|content_block_start|content_block_delta|content_block_stop)
            ;;

        *)
            # Unknown type - skip silently unless debug
            ;;
    esac

    # Periodic summary
    check_summary_needed
}

# =============================================================================
# MAIN
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Check for jq
    if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} jq not found - passing through raw output" >&2
        echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} Install: brew install jq (macOS) / apt install jq (Linux)" >&2
        cat
        return 0
    fi

    # Trap for clean exit with summary
    trap 'echo ""; print_summary; exit 130' INT TERM

    # Process stdin line by line
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        process_line "$line"
    done

    # Final summary
    print_summary
}

# Run if executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# pre-iteration.sh - Ralph pre-iteration validation hook
# =============================================================================
# Runs before each Ralph iteration to check state and warn about issues.
# Always exits 0 (informational only, never blocks iteration).
#
# Called from: ralph/loop.sh (before Claude call)
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Resolve project directory
PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || { return 0 2>/dev/null || true; }

# Ralph directory
RALPH_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/ralph"
PLAN_FILE="$RALPH_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"
FIX_PLAN="$RALPH_DIR/@fix_plan.md"

# Thresholds
MAX_PLAN_LINES=${MAX_PLAN_LINES:-400}
STALE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS=${STALE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS:-3600}

# =============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

get_file_age_seconds() {
    local file="$1"
    local file_mtime
    local current_time

    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        echo "0"
        return
    fi

    current_time=$(date +%s)

    # Cross-platform file modification time
    if stat --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        # GNU stat (Linux)
        file_mtime=$(stat -c %Y "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "$current_time")
    else
        # BSD stat (macOS)
        file_mtime=$(stat -f %m "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "$current_time")
    fi

    echo $((current_time - file_mtime))
}

format_duration() {
    local seconds="$1"
    local hours=$((seconds / 3600))
    local minutes=$(((seconds % 3600) / 60))

    if [[ $hours -gt 0 ]]; then
        echo "${hours}h ${minutes}m"
    else
        echo "${minutes}m"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# CHECKS
# =============================================================================

# Check 1: Plan size
if [[ -f "$PLAN_FILE" ]]; then
    PLAN_LINES=$(wc -l < "$PLAN_FILE" | tr -d ' ')

    if [[ $PLAN_LINES -gt $MAX_PLAN_LINES ]]; then
        echo "[WARN] IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md has $PLAN_LINES lines (max: $MAX_PLAN_LINES)"
        echo "[WARN] Consider: ./ralph/scripts/archive-completed.sh"
    fi
fi

# Check 2: Stale @fix_plan.md
if [[ -f "$FIX_PLAN" ]]; then
    AGE_SECONDS=$(get_file_age_seconds "$FIX_PLAN")

    if [[ $AGE_SECONDS -gt $STALE_THRESHOLD_SECONDS ]]; then
        AGE_FORMATTED=$(format_duration "$AGE_SECONDS")
        echo "[WARN] @fix_plan.md is $AGE_FORMATTED old - may be stuck"
        echo "[WARN] Review and complete or delete if no longer needed"
    fi
fi

# Always succeed (informational only)
# Use return instead of exit - this script is sourced, exit would kill the parent shell
return 0 2>/dev/null || true

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# session-start.sh - Ralph session initialization hook
# =============================================================================
# Displays project info, plan status, and alerts at Claude session start.
# Always exits 0 (informational only, never blocks session).
#
# Called from: .claude/settings.json SessionStart hook
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# Resolve project directory
PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
cd "$PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || { return 0 2>/dev/null || true; }

# Ralph directory
RALPH_DIR="$PROJECT_DIR/ralph"
PLAN_FILE="$RALPH_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"
FIX_PLAN="$RALPH_DIR/@fix_plan.md"

# =============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# =============================================================================

count_plan_tasks() {
    local status="$1"
    local file="$2"

    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        echo "0"
        return
    fi

    case "$status" in
        completed)
            grep -c '^\s*-\s*\[x\]' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
            ;;
        pending)
            grep -c '^\s*-\s*\[\s*\]' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
            ;;
        in_progress)
            grep -c 'IN PROGRESS' "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
            ;;
    esac
}

get_plan_lines() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        wc -l < "$file" | tr -d ' '
    else
        echo "0"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# GATHER INFO
# =============================================================================

# Project name from package.json or directory name
PROJECT_NAME=""
if [[ -f "$PROJECT_DIR/package.json" ]]; then
    PROJECT_NAME=$(grep -o '"name":\s*"[^"]*"' "$PROJECT_DIR/package.json" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
fi
[[ -z "$PROJECT_NAME" ]] && PROJECT_NAME=$(basename "$PROJECT_DIR")

# Git info
GIT_BRANCH=$(git -C "$PROJECT_DIR" branch --show-current 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")

# Plan metrics
PLAN_LINES=$(get_plan_lines "$PLAN_FILE")
PLAN_COMPLETED=$(count_plan_tasks "completed" "$PLAN_FILE")
PLAN_PENDING=$(count_plan_tasks "pending" "$PLAN_FILE")

# =============================================================================
# OUTPUT
# =============================================================================

# Project header
echo "$PROJECT_NAME | $GIT_BRANCH | $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')"

# Plan status
if [[ -f "$PLAN_FILE" ]]; then
    echo "Plan: ${PLAN_LINES}L | Done:$PLAN_COMPLETED Pending:$PLAN_PENDING"

    # Warn if plan is large
    if [[ $PLAN_LINES -gt 400 ]]; then
        echo "ALERT: Plan >400 lines - run ./ralph/scripts/archive-completed.sh"
    fi
else
    echo "Plan: NOT FOUND"
fi

# Check for @fix_plan.md (priority interrupt)
if [[ -f "$FIX_PLAN" ]]; then
    echo "ALERT: @fix_plan.md exists - MUST complete before normal tasks"
fi

# Check for blockers in plan
if [[ -f "$PLAN_FILE" ]]; then
    if grep -q '## BLOCKERS' "$PLAN_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
        # Check if blockers section has actual task items (not just "_None currently_")
        BLOCKER_CONTENT=$(sed -n '/## BLOCKERS/,/^---$/p' "$PLAN_FILE" 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^\s*-\s*\[' 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)
        if [[ -n "$BLOCKER_CONTENT" ]]; then
            echo "ALERT: Active blockers in plan - resolve first"
        fi
    fi
fi

# Always succeed (informational only)
# Use return instead of exit - this script may be sourced, exit would kill the parent shell
return 0 2>/dev/null || true

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# pre-iteration.sh - Pre-iteration checks for Ralph loop
# =============================================================================
# Sourced by loop.sh before each Claude call. Validates state and warns
# about potential issues that could affect the iteration.
#
# =============================================================================

# Script directory (when sourced, use RALPH_DIR if set)
SCRIPT_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
RALPH_DIR="${RALPH_DIR:-$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")}"

# Files
PLAN_FILE="$RALPH_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"
FIX_PLAN_FILE="$RALPH_DIR/@fix_plan.md"

# Thresholds
MAX_LINES=${MAX_LINES:-400}

# Check plan size
if [[ -f "$PLAN_FILE" ]]; then
    LINES=$(wc -l < "$PLAN_FILE" | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ $LINES -gt $MAX_LINES ]]; then
        echo "[WARN] IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md has $LINES lines (max: $MAX_LINES)"
        echo "[WARN] Consider: ./ralph/scripts/archive-completed.sh"
    fi
fi

# Check for stale @fix_plan.md (older than 1 hour)
if [[ -f "$FIX_PLAN_FILE" ]]; then
    # Cross-platform stat for modification time
    if [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
        MOD_TIME=$(stat -f %m "$FIX_PLAN_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    else
        MOD_TIME=$(stat -c %Y "$FIX_PLAN_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    fi

    NOW=$(date +%s)
    AGE=$((NOW - MOD_TIME))

    if [[ $AGE -gt 3600 ]]; then
        echo "[WARN] @fix_plan.md is over 1 hour old ($(($AGE / 60)) min) - may be stuck"
    fi
fi

#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# post-iteration.sh - Post-iteration metrics for Ralph loop
# =============================================================================
# Sourced by loop.sh after each Claude call. Logs metrics and checks if
# maintenance actions are needed.
#
# =============================================================================

# Script directory (when sourced, use RALPH_DIR if set)
SCRIPT_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)}"
RALPH_DIR="${RALPH_DIR:-$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")}"
PROJECT_ROOT="${PROJECT_ROOT:-$(dirname "$RALPH_DIR")}"

# Files
PLAN_FILE="$RALPH_DIR/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md"

# Thresholds
WARN_LINES=${WARN_LINES:-350}

# Log commits today
COMMITS_TODAY=$(git -C "$PROJECT_ROOT" log --oneline --since="midnight" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
echo "[INFO] Commits today: $COMMITS_TODAY"

# Check if archival is approaching
if [[ -f "$PLAN_FILE" ]]; then
    PLAN_LINES=$(wc -l < "$PLAN_FILE" | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ $PLAN_LINES -gt $WARN_LINES ]]; then
        echo "[INFO] Plan at $PLAN_LINES lines - approaching limit (400)"
    fi
fi
